Please , dows exist a component in WPF c# 5.0 that use a customization datetime in a fotmat "mm/yyyy"?If the answer is positive,please, how to use it?

In WPF, you can use the `DatePicker` control to display a date and the `StringFormat` property to customize the format string.

To display the date in the format "MM/yyyy", you can set the `StringFormat` property of the `DatePicker` to `"MM/yyyy"`. Here's an example XAML code:

```xml
<DatePicker SelectedDate="{Binding MyDate}" StringFormat="MM/yyyy" />
```

In the above code, `MyDate` is a property in your view model that stores the date value selected by the user.

You can also set the `DatePickerTextBox` style to specify a custom `ControlTemplate` to further customize the appearance of the `DatePicker` control. Here's an example style:

```xml
<Style TargetType="DatePickerTextBox">
    <Setter Property="Template">
        <Setter.Value>
            <ControlTemplate TargetType="DatePickerTextBox">
                <TextBox x:Name="PART_TextBox"
                         Text="{Binding Path=SelectedDate, RelativeSource={RelativeSource AncestorType={x:Type DatePicker}}, StringFormat='MM/yyyy'}"
                         BorderThickness="0"
                         Background="Transparent"/>
            </ControlTemplate>
        </Setter.Value>
    </Setter>
</Style>
```

In the above style, the `PART_TextBox` element is bound to the selected date of the `DatePicker` using the `SelectedDate` property and the `RelativeSource` binding. The `StringFormat` property is used to set the format string of the displayed date. The `Background` and `BorderThickness` are set to make the `TextBox` transparent and borderless.

You can apply the style to the `DatePicker` control using the `Style` property:

```xml
<DatePicker SelectedDate="{Binding MyDate}" Style="{StaticResource MyDatePickerStyle}" />
```

In the above code, `MyDatePickerStyle` is the name of the custom style defined earlier.
How to customize a date using a wpf c# 4.5.1 control that uses "mm/yyyy" format?

To display a date in the format "mm/yyyy" using a WPF control in C# 4.5.1, there are a few options available.

1. `DatePicker`: You can use the `DatePicker` control and set the `SelectedDateFormat` property to `DatePickerFormat.Custom`, and then set the `CustomFormat` property to `"MM/yyyy"`. Here's an example:

```xml
<DatePicker x:Name="datePicker" SelectedDate="{Binding MyDate}" SelectedDateFormat="Custom" CustomFormat="MM/yyyy" />
```

In the above code, `MyDate` is a property in your view model that stores the date value selected by the user.

2. `TextBox`: You can use a `TextBox` control and set the `Text` property to a formatted string. Here's an example:

```xml
<TextBox x:Name="dateTextBox" Text="{Binding MyDate, StringFormat=MM/yyyy}" />
```

In the above code, `MyDate` is a property in your view model that stores the date value. The `StringFormat` property is used to format the displayed date string.

3. `MaskedTextBox` (using `Microsoft.VisualBasic` library): You can use a `MaskedTextBox` control from `Microsoft.VisualBasic` library and set the `Mask` property to `"00/0000"`. Here's an example:

```xml
<WindowsFormsHost>
    <vb:MaskedTextBox x:Name="maskedTextBox" Text="{Binding MyDate}" Mask="00/0000" />
</WindowsFormsHost>
```

In the above code, `MyDate` is a property in your view model that stores the date value. The `Mask` property is used to define the format of the displayed date string.

Note that the `MaskedTextBox` is a Windows Forms control and needs to be hosted inside a `WindowsFormsHost` element in WPF. Also, you need to add a reference to the `Microsoft.VisualBasic` library to use the `MaskedTextBox` control.
How to reference a vb MasketTextBox in a xaml wpf c#?

To reference a `MaskedTextBox` control from `Microsoft.VisualBasic` library in a XAML WPF C# project, you need to do the following steps:

1. Add a reference to the `Microsoft.VisualBasic` library by right-clicking on your project in the Solution Explorer, selecting "Add Reference", and selecting the `Microsoft.VisualBasic` assembly from the ".NET" tab.

2. In your XAML code, add a namespace reference to the `Microsoft.VisualBasic` library by including the following `xmlns:vb` attribute in your XAML root element:

```xml
xmlns:vb="clr-namespace:Microsoft.VisualBasic;assembly=Microsoft.VisualBasic"
```

3. Use the `WindowsFormsHost` control to host the `MaskedTextBox` control inside your XAML code:

```xml
<Window x:Class="WpfApp1.MainWindow"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
    xmlns:vb="clr-namespace:Microsoft.VisualBasic;assembly=Microsoft.VisualBasic"
    xmlns:wf="clr-namespace:System.Windows.Forms;assembly=System.Windows.Forms"
    Title="MainWindow" Height="350" Width="525">
    <Grid>
        <WindowsFormsHost>
            <vb:MaskedTextBox x:Name="maskedTextBox" Mask="00/0000" Text="01/2021" />
        </WindowsFormsHost>
    </Grid>
</Window>
```

In the above code, the `WindowsFormsHost` control is used to host the `MaskedTextBox` control from the `Microsoft.VisualBasic` library. The `Mask` property is set to `"00/0000"` to specify the required format of the date string, and the `Text` property is set to `"01/2021"` to initialize the date value.

Note that in the above code, the `WindowsFormsHost` control is hosted inside a `Grid` container. You may need to adjust the layout of your XAML code based on your specific needs.
How to customize a date using a wpf c# 4.5.1 control that uses “mm/yyyy” format?

To display a date in "mm/yyyy" format using a WPF control in C# 4.5.1, you can use the following options:

1. `DatePicker` control: You can use the built-in `DatePicker` control in WPF and set the `SelectedDateFormat` property as `DatePickerFormat.Custom`. After that, set the `CustomFormat` property as `"MM/yyyy"`. Here's an example:

```xml
<DatePicker x:Name="datePicker" SelectedDate="{Binding MyDate}" SelectedDateFormat="Custom" CustomFormat="MM/yyyy" />
```

In the above code, `MyDate` is a property in your view model that holds the date value.

2. `TextBox` control: You can use the `TextBox` control and bind the `Text` property to the `MyDate` property of the view model. During binding, you can set the `StringFormat` property as `"MM/yyyy"`. Here's an example:

```xml
<TextBox x:Name="dateTextBox" Text="{Binding MyDate, StringFormat=MM/yyyy}" />
```

In the above code, `MyDate` is a property in your view model that holds the date value.

3. `MaskedTextBox` control: You can use the `MaskedTextBox` control from the `Microsoft.VisualBasic` library and bind the `Text` property to the `MyDate` property of the view model. You can set the `Mask` property as `"00/0000"` to specify the date format. Here's an example:

```xml
<WindowsFormsHost>
    <vb:MaskedTextBox x:Name="maskedTextBox" Text="{Binding MyDate}" Mask="00/0000" />
</WindowsFormsHost>
```

In the above code, `MyDate` is a property in your view model that holds the date value. As `MaskedTextBox` belongs to the Windows Forms controls, you have to host it within a `WindowsFormsHost` control. Additionally, you have to include `xmlns:wf="clr-namespace:System.Windows.Forms;assembly=System.Windows.Forms"` in XAML to reference the System.Windows.Forms library.

Note: For the last option, you have to add a reference to the `Microsoft.VisualBasic` and `System.Windows.Forms` libraries.
I need to customize a wpf c# component using date format "MM/yyyy".How to do it??

To display a date in the format of "MM/yyyy" in a WPF C# component, there are different options available depending on the control you want to use.

Here is how to customize a few commonly used WPF C# controls:

1. `DatePicker`: You can use the built-in `DatePicker` control in WPF and set the `SelectedDateFormat` property as `DatePickerFormat.Custom`. After that, set the `CustomFormat` property as `"MM/yyyy"`. Here's how to do it:

```xml
<DatePicker x:Name="datePicker" SelectedDate="{Binding MyDate}" SelectedDateFormat="Custom" CustomFormat="MM/yyyy" />
```

In the above code, `MyDate` is a property in the view model that holds the date value.

2. `TextBox`: You can use the `TextBox` control and bind the `Text` property to the `MyDate` property of the view model. During binding, you can set the `StringFormat` property as `"MM/yyyy"`. Here's an example:

```xml
<TextBox x:Name="dateTextBox" Text="{Binding MyDate, StringFormat=MM/yyyy}" />
```

In the above code, `MyDate` is a property in the view model that holds the date value.

3. `DataGrid`: If you want to display dates in the format of "MM/yyyy" in a `DataGrid`, you can customize the `DataGridTextColumn` control. Here's how to do it:

```xml
<DataGridTextColumn Header="Date" Binding="{Binding MyDate, StringFormat=MM/yyyy}" />
```

In the above code, `MyDate` is a property in the view model that holds the date value.

4. `TextBlock`: You can use the `TextBlock` control and set the `Text` property to the formatted date string using the `StringFormat` property. Here's an example:

```xml
<TextBlock Text="{Binding MyDate, StringFormat=MM/yyyy}" />
```

In the above code, `MyDate` is a property in the view model that holds the date value.

Note that for the `DatePicker` control and the `DataGridTextColumn` control, you have to specify `SelectedDateFormat` and `Binding` respectively to enable customization. For `TextBox`, `DataGrid`, and `TextBlock` controls, you can set the `StringFormat` property to customize the date format.
how to customize a date using "MM/YYYY" a textbox wpf c# control on code?
how to customize data in a format "MM/YYYY" using textblock c# wpf

To customize the display format of a `DateTime` value in a `TextBlock` control as "MM/YYYY" in WPF C#, you can use the `TextBlock.Text` property and the `DateTime.ToString` method with the `"MM/yyyy"` format string. Here is an example:

```csharp
DateTime date = DateTime.Now;
textBlock.Text = date.ToString("MM/yyyy");
```

In the above code, `textBlock` is the name of the `TextBlock` control to be customized. The `ToString` method is used to format the date value as a string with the specified format. The `"MM/yyyy"` is the format specifier for the month and year components of the date.

If you are binding a `DateTime` property in your view model to a `TextBlock`, you can set the `StringFormat` property in your binding expression to specify the date format. Here is an example:

```xml
<TextBlock Text="{Binding MyDate, StringFormat=MM/yyyy}" />
```

In the above code, `MyDate` is a property in your view model that holds the date value. The `StringFormat` attribute is used to specify the date format. Note that the `StringFormat` attribute is only available in WPF 4.5 and later versions. If you are using an earlier version of WPF, you will need to create a value converter to format the date value as a string with the specified format.

I put a MaskedTextBox on my wpf c# usercontrol.When I run the application this maskedTextBox doesn't show.What could it happened?

If you are using a `MaskedTextBox` control from the `Microsoft.VisualBasic` library in a WPF C# project, you need to host it inside a WPF `WindowsFormsHost` control. Here's an example:

```xml
<WindowsFormsHost>
    <vb:MaskedTextBox Mask="00/00/0000" x:Name="maskedTextBox" Text="01/01/2022" />
</WindowsFormsHost>
```

In the above code, `vb` is the namespace for the `Microsoft.VisualBasic` library. `MaskedTextBox` is the name of the control, and `Mask` and `Text` are the properties used to set the mask and a default value, respectively.

Note that hosting a Windows Forms control inside WPF may cause some visual artifacts. You can create a custom control or use a third-party control to achieve the same functionality in a more WPF-friendly way.

If you have already hosted the `MaskedTextBox` control inside a `WindowsFormsHost` control, then you may need to check if the control is visible. You can set the `Visibility` property of the `WindowsFormsHost` control to `Visible` if you want to show the control. Here's an example:

```xml
<WindowsFormsHost Visibility="Visible">
    <vb:MaskedTextBox Mask="00/00/0000" x:Name="maskedTextBox" Text="01/01/2022" />
</WindowsFormsHost>
```

In the above code, the `Visibility` property of the `WindowsFormsHost` control is set to `Visible`.

Alternatively, you can also check if there are any layout or positioning issues with the `WindowsFormsHost` control and adjust them as necessary.

```csharp
string pattern = @"^\d{2}/\d{4}$";
```
